beats.text: You proved that when you change the order of the factors, the product stays the same. You used highlighting, rotation, and turn-around facts. Let's see what you know.

incorrects.description: Student placed incorrect tiles on first attempt for row equation

steps.text: Not quite. Count the rows — the horizontal bands.

incorrects.description: Student placed incorrect tiles on second attempt for row equation

params.description: One row pulses to show what counts as a row.

steps.text: Let's think about this together. The rows are highlighted. There are 6 rows. Each row has 7 dots.

incorrects.description: Student placed incorrect tiles three or more times. System models the answer.

params.description: System places tiles 6, 7, and 42 into slots.

steps.text: Let me show you how this works. I count the rows: 6. I count the dots in one row: 7. So the equation is 6 times 7 equals 42.

incorrects.description: Student placed incorrect tiles on first attempt for column equation

steps.text: Check the columns — the vertical bands.

incorrects.description: Student placed incorrect tiles on second attempt for column equation

params.description: One column pulses to show what counts as a column.

steps.text: Here's a hint: the columns are highlighted. Count them. There are 7 columns. Each column has 6 dots.

incorrects.description: Student placed incorrect tiles three or more times. System models the answer.

params.description: System places tiles 7, 6, and 42 into slots.

steps.text: Here, let me walk you through this one. I count the columns: 7. I count the dots in one column: 6. So the equation is 7 times 6 equals 42. The product stays the same.

incorrects.description: Student selected 2 × 8: did not recognize that rotation changes the row expression

params.description: Array highlights to show current orientation: 2 rows of 8.

steps.text: That's the expression now, before rotating. When we rotate, rows become columns and columns become rows. What will the new row count be?

incorrects.description: Student selected 16 × 2: used the total instead of the row structure

params.description: Array highlights to emphasize row structure.

steps.text: 16 is the total dots, not the number of rows. After rotation, the 8 columns will become 8 rows. How many dots will be in each row?

incorrects.description: Student selected 8 + 2: used addition instead of multiplication

params.description: Array highlights rows to show multiplication structure.

steps.text: Arrays use multiplication, not addition. We count rows times dots per row. After rotation, we'll have 8 rows. How many dots will each row have?

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: Array now shows 8 rows of 2 dots each.

steps.text: Let me show you how rotation works. The 2 rows become 2 columns. The 8 columns become 8 rows. Now we have 8 rows of 2 dots. The row expression is 8 times 2. Rotation switches the numbers in the multiplication.

incorrects.description: Student selected A) 3 × 10, which has 30 dots but different row and column structure

steps.text: That array has 30 dots, but look at the rows. The reference has 5 rows. When you rotate, the rows become columns.

incorrects.description: Student selected C) 10 × 3, which has 30 dots but different row and column structure

steps.text: This array has 30 dots, but the row and column counts are different. When you rotate 5 rows of 6, you get 6 rows of 5.

incorrects.description: Student selected D) 2 × 15, which has 30 dots but completely different structure

steps.text: That array has 30 dots, but rotating doesn't change the numbers that much. If you turn 5 rows of 6, the 5 and the 6 swap places.

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: Reference array rotates 90 degrees, transforming from 5 rows of 6 to 6 rows of 5.

steps.text: Let me show you how this works. When I rotate the reference array, the 5 rows become 5 columns. The 6 dots in each row become 6 rows. So 5 rows of 6 becomes 6 rows of 5. The answer is B) 6 × 5. That is the rotated version of the reference array.
